title: Caatinganthus rubropappus
taxonomy: Caatinganthus rubropappus is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is native to Brazil and is commonly found in the Caatinga biome. This species is classified as a flowering plant, belonging to the class Magnoliopsida. The classification further breaks down into the order Asterales, family Asteraceae, genus Caatinganthus, and species Caatinganthus rubropappus. The Caatinganthus rubropappus is a member of the eudicot clade within the angiosperm clade.
uses: Caatinganthus rubropappus has been traditionally used in folk medicine, primarily for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy in treating various health conditions. Further investigation is necessary to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses and applications.

intro: Caatinganthus rubropappus, also known as the Caatinga mallow, is a plant species native to the Caatinga dry forest in Brazil, characterized by its showy, red-orange flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and improve overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Caatinganthus rubropappus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Caatinganthus rubropappus is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote relaxation.
